title: ageratina aromatica var. aromatica
intro: Ageratina aromatica var. aromatica, also known as Mexican oregano, is a shrub-like perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly carvacrol and thymol. The health benefits of Mexican oregano include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support respiratory health, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to food as a spice to take advantage of its flavor and medicinal properties.
